mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically elevating equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment rates reflect weak aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate revenue expectations, which immediately suppress short-term equity momentum and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in over 65% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts reading below 500 thousand units signals a severe contraction in residential construction demand, which cascades into lower capital goods orders and reduced economic growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear quarters with a t-statistic above 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index exceeding 95.0 indicates a robust manufacturing expansion that outpaces baseline growth forecasts, prompting institutional portfolio rebalancing toward equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a mean difference of less than 0.5% between above-threshold and below-threshold periods, or Rank IC is negative over a 24-month walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of near-zero federal funds rates and declining unemployment creates a synchronized liquidity and growth tailwind that amplifies equity risk premiums, producing strongly positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and UNRATE yields a coefficient insignificant at the 5% level in a multivariate regression of 20-day returns, or combined signal accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and acceleration in consumer price inflation signals stagflationary pressure that forces central banks to restrict liquidity, compressing valuation multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in over 60% of observations when both GDPC1 growth slows and CPIAUCSL rises, or the interaction effect fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by more than 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls during a confirmed economic expansion regime reflect strengthening consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings revisions and triggers immediate positive S&P 500 momentum over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day returns during expansion regimes, or the signal directional accuracy falls below 48% in a 3-year rolling validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real short-term interest rates during a monetary accommodation regime reduce the carry on cash positions, forcing leveraged funds to deleverage equity portfolios and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of declining RSAFS observations within the accommodation regime, or the signal Rank IC is consistently negative across all rolling windows.
